question_html:
Identify the type of gender of the underlined words in the following sentences:

(a) The Chairman arrived on time at the meeting.

(b) They hired a companion for their grandmother.

(c) The horse cleared the hurdle.

(d) We rarely get to see any milkmaid today.

(e) His computer is out of order.
solution_html:
(a) masculine (b) common (c) masculine (d) feminine (e) neuter

Marks Distribution:

For identifying the correct type of gender of the underlined words (0.5×5) =2.5 marks.

[N.B. Any kind of mistake bears no marks. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.]

question_html:
Fill in the blanks with suitable words:

The 21st century has witnessed rapid (a) ____ in technology, profoundly transforming industries and societies worldwide. Innovations such as artificial intelligence and blockchain have introduced (b) ____ methods of processing data, creating opportunities for efficiency and security. However, these advances also present (c) ____ challenges, including ethical concerns and cybersecurity threats that require global (d) ____. Governments and private sectors must collaborate to develop (e) ____ regulations that balance innovation with safety. Furthermore, fostering (f) ____ among workers is essential to adapt to evolving job markets. Educational systems need to focus on (g) ____ skills that emphasize critical thinking and creativity. Amidst these transformations, maintaining environmental (h) ____ is crucial to sustainable development. International cooperation and (i) ____ policies will be pivotal in addressing climate change and resource management. Ultimately, the future depends on our ability to implement (j) ____ solutions that are both technologically advanced and socially responsible.
solution_html:
(a) advancement / progression / evolution / innovation / development / progress
‎(b) sophisticated / novel / revolutionary / cutting-edge / advanced / innovative
‎(c) complex / formidable / unprecedented / multifaceted / daunting / significant
‎(d) cooperation / coordination / collaboration / consensus / partnership
‎(e) stringent / comprehensive / effective / pragmatic / balanced / appropriate
‎(f) adaptability / resilience / flexibility / versatility / openness / future-oriented / practical
‎(g) transferable / cognitive / interdisciplinary / analytical / problem-solving
‎(h) stewardship / conservation / sustainability / preservation / protection / balance

(i) inclusive / coherent / forward-looking / integrative / proactive / strong
‎(j) sustainable / pragmatic / innovative / equitable / scalable / responsible

Marks Distribution:

For writing 10 suitable words, (0.25×10) =2.5 marks.

[N.B. Any kind of mistake bears no marks. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.]

question_html:
Sundays too my father got up early
‎ and put his clothes on in the blueblack cold,
‎ then with cracked hands that ached
‎ from labor in the weekday weather made
‎ banked fires blaze. No one ever thanked him.
‎ I'd wake and hear the cold splintering, breaking.
‎ When the rooms were warm, he'd call,
‎ and slowly I would rise and dress,
‎ fearing the chronic angers of that house.

What is the name of the poem? Who is the poet?
‎Write down the summary.
solution_html:
The name of the poem is ‘Those Winter Sundays’ by
‎Robert Hayden.

In ‘Those Winter Sundays’, the poet reflects on his father’s silent sacrifices, waking early on cold Sundays to warm the house despite painful, worn hands. The father’s efforts go unnoticed and unthanked, while the speaker recalls the tense household atmosphere and his own fear. The poem explores themes of parental love, sacrifice, and regret.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the name of the poem, 01 mark.

For writing the poet, 01 mark.

Mentionable two sentences, (1.5×2) = 03 marks.

In Robert Hayden’s ‘Those Winter Sundays’, the poem highlights the father’s quiet sacrifices and unacknowledged love, shown through his early rising and labor in cold weather to care for the family.

Ultimately, it reflects the speaker’s regret and realization of the deep but silent affection behind his father’s harsh demeanor.

Instructions:

(i) It has to be within 55 words (But the students can write it less than the mentioned words)

(ii) If it becomes more than 55 words but less than double (110), 01 marks have to be deducted.

(iii) If it becomes double (110 words), 02 marks have to be deducted from the deserving marks.

Comment: Write not more than one third of the text/ Write within one third of the text.

(iv) If it becomes “copied”, rather than creative/own writings, marks have to be deducted depending on the degree of the copied portion. For every (01) copied sentence, 0.5 has to be deducted. But if the student copies the whole summary, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

Comment: Copied from the text (The examiners have to underline the copied portion)

(v) If a student writes double (110 words) than the mentioned words and copies the whole summary, the examiner will deduct the marks following any of the two formats.

[N.B: For one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes 0.5 mark has to be deducted.

For every 03 (spelling or grammatical) mistakes 01 mark has to be deducted.
